per_turn_leaks.student_message: Nocturnal diarrhea is a red flag — it suggests an organic rather than functional cause. With that many bowel movements, do you feel like you're fully emptying each time, or is there a sensation of incomplete evacuation?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I don't really get that feeling of being fully emptied after most bowel movements. It's like my gut just feels... unfinished, even when I'm done going to the bathroom. (refers to G05)

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Tenesmus with bloody diarrhea points strongly toward an inflammatory process in the rectum and colon. With this level of GI inflammation, the body often responds systemically. Have you had any fevers or chills?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: I've been feeling a bit achy all over, but it's mostly my knees and ankles that hurt. It's not like I'm swollen or anything, but when I move around, it feels pretty sore. And yeah, sometimes I get this low-grade fever – nothing too high, just around 98-99°F (around 36.7-37.2°C) a few times a week.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Low-grade fevers fit the pattern. Inflammatory bowel disease often has extraintestinal manifestations — the same inflammatory process can affect joints. Have you noticed any joint pain or stiffness?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: Yeah, that's right. The joint pain is pretty consistent – it's achy in my knees and ankles, no swelling or redness. It's not super severe, but it's definitely there all the time, especially after I've been sitting for a while. (refers to G07)

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Ibuprofen is important to note — NSAIDs can actually worsen inflammatory bowel disease. That might be contributing to your flare. With bloody diarrhea eight to ten times a day for three weeks, you must be losing weight. Have you noticed any changes on the scale?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, I have lost some weight – about 4 kg (9 lbs) over the past 3 weeks. It's not something I'm super concerned about right now, given everything else that's going on. But yeah, my clothes are feeling a bit looser than they used to.

per_turn_leaks.student_message: Four kilos in three weeks is significant — that's consistent with the severity of your symptoms. You mentioned you work long hours at a startup. That kind of stress can trigger and worsen GI inflammation. How are you coping with all of this?
per_turn_leaks.patient_response: To be honest, it's been really tough lately. The stress from work has just been piling up on top of these symptoms, and it feels like everything is getting worse by the day. I've actually started drinking a bit more beer to try and cope with the anxiety and stress – 4-5 beers per night, which is way above my usual.
